subject: cs
prompt: Generate 5 diagram-based questions on neuromorphic computing and spiking neural networks. Cover: leaky integrate-and-fire (LIF) neuron model with membrane potential trace, spike-timing dependent plasticity (STDP) learning rule graph, crossbar array architecture for synaptic weights, the temporal coding scheme in a spiking network, and comparison of energy consumption between von Neumann and neuromorphic architectures. Each question must require the student to analyze or interpret a diagram.

questions.question: Analyze the diagram below of a leaky integrate-and-fire (LIF) neuron model that includes a plot of the membrane potential over time. Describe the key phases of the potential change, identify the threshold crossing, and explain the impact of the leak current on neuron firing.

diagram.description: CUMULATIVE LEAK CORRECTIONS (all must be fixed): Known answer terms to avoid: threshold; The diagram explicitly labels the "Threshold" in the legend, which is a forbidden label and directly answers the part of the question asking the student to identify the threshold crossing.; The diagram explicitly labels the "Threshold" in the legend, which directly answers a part of the question asking the student to identify the threshold crossing.. A 2D plot titled 'Leaky Integrate-and-Fire Neuron Model'. The x-axis is labeled 'Time (ms)' and the y-axis is labeled 'Membrane Potential (mV)'. The plot must show the membrane potential trace (solid black line), a dotted line for the resting potential, and a dashed red line for the threshold level. The legend should include entries for 'Membrane Potential' and 'Rest

questions.correctAnswer: In the diagram below, the membrane potential initially increases due to incoming spikes, as shown by the upward trend in the plot. It then decreases due to the leak current, which is depicted by the gradual decline in potential, until it either resets (if not reaching the threshold) or spikes if it surpasses the threshold, as indicated by the sharp upward peak. The leak current stabilizes the membrane potential, regulating neuron firing. The threshold crossing, marked in the diagram, represents the firing event.
questions.rubric: Award 3 points for correctly identifying the phases of potential change (excitation and leak) as depicted in the diagram. Award 3 points for correctly identifying the threshold crossing as shown in the diagram. Award 4 points for explaining the role of the leak current, as illustrated in the diagram.
questions.text: Analyze the diagram below of a leaky integrate-and-fire (LIF) neuron model that includes a plot of the membrane potential over time. Describe the key phases of the potential change, identify the threshold crossing, and explain the impact of the leak current on neuron firing.

questions.question: Examine the diagram below of the spike-timing dependent plasticity (STDP) learning rule. Discuss how the timing difference between presynaptic and postsynaptic spikes affects synaptic weight changes.

diagram.description:
STDP learning rule graph showing synaptic weight change versus timing difference between presynaptic and postsynaptic spikes. The x-axis represents the timing difference (Δt) in milliseconds, with negative values indicating presynaptic spikes occurring before postsynaptic spikes, and positive values indicating the opposite. The y-axis represents the change in synaptic weight, with positive values indicating potentiation and negative values indicating depression. The curve typically shows a sharp increase in weight for small negative Δt and a decrease for small positive Δt, illustrating the timing-dependent nature of synaptic plasticity.

FORBIDDEN LABELS (do NOT render as text, legend entries, annotations, note boxes, or titles — these reveal the answer):
  • depression
  • potentiation
The diagram will FAIL review if any of these appear.

questions.correctAnswer: Synaptic weights increase (potentiation) when the presynaptic spike precedes the postsynaptic spike, and decrease (depression) when the presynaptic spike follows the postsynaptic spike. This is illustrated in the diagram below by the positive slope on the left and negative slope on the right of the curve.
questions.rubric: Award 5 points for correctly identifying potentiation when presynaptic spikes precede postsynaptic spikes, as shown in the diagram. Award 5 points for correctly identifying depression when presynaptic spikes follow postsynaptic spikes, as illustrated in the diagram below.
questions.text: Examine the diagram below of the spike-timing dependent plasticity (STDP) learning rule. Discuss how the timing difference between presynaptic and postsynaptic spikes affects synaptic weight changes.

questions.question: Review the diagram below of a crossbar array architecture for synaptic weights. Explain how synaptic pruning can occur through this architecture and its potential benefits for spiking neural networks.

diagram.description:
Crossbar array architecture for synaptic weights. The diagram should show a grid of horizontal and vertical lines representing the crossbar structure. Each intersection point represents a synapse, which can be selectively connected or disconnected to simulate synaptic pruning. Label the horizontal lines as 'Input Lines' and the vertical lines as 'Output Lines'. Highlight a few intersections to indicate active synapses. Include arrows showing the flow of signals from input to output lines. This setup will help explain how synaptic pruning can occur in the architecture.

FORBIDDEN LABELS (do NOT render as text, legend entries, annotations, note boxes, or titles — these reveal the answer):
  • threshold
The diagram will FAIL review if any of these appear. IMPORTANT: The diagram is missing required labels. Specifically: Missing label: 'Input Lines', Missing label: 'Output Lines' CORRECTED. The diagram uses the labels "Input Neurons" and "Output Neurons" instead of the required labels "Input Lines" and "Output Lines" as specified in the diagram description.. Add each missing label as visible text at the appropriate location in the diagram. Ensure ALL labels are fully visible and not clipped by diagram boundaries. Use smaller font or reposition labels near edges if needed.

questions.correctAnswer: Synaptic pruning in the crossbar array, as illustrated in the diagram below, occurs when the synaptic weights fall below a threshold, effectively disconnecting low-weight connections. This process, depicted in the circuit shown, reduces energy consumption and increases network efficiency by focusing resources on stronger, more significant connections.
questions.rubric: Award 5 points for describing synaptic pruning through thresholding in the crossbar as shown in the diagram. Award 5 points for explaining benefits like reduced energy consumption and increased efficiency.
questions.text: Review the diagram below of a crossbar array architecture for synaptic weights. Explain how synaptic pruning can occur through this architecture and its potential benefits for spiking neural networks.

questions.question: Evaluate the effectiveness of the temporal coding scheme in a spiking neural network as depicted in the diagram below. Discuss how this coding scheme can enhance information encoding compared to rate coding.

diagram.description: A diagram showing a spiking neural network with temporal coding

questions.correctAnswer: Temporal coding, as illustrated in the diagram below, captures the precise timing of spikes, allowing the network to convey more information with fewer spikes compared to rate coding. The circuit shown demonstrates how this coding scheme enhances information capacity and enables more dynamic and rapid information processing.
questions.rubric: Award 5 points for correctly explaining the concept of temporal coding as depicted in the diagram. Award 5 points for discussing its advantages over rate coding, such as increased information capacity and faster processing, in the context of the circuit shown.
questions.text: Evaluate the effectiveness of the temporal coding scheme in a spiking neural network as depicted in the diagram below. Discuss how this coding scheme can enhance information encoding compared to rate coding.

questions.correctAnswer: Neuromorphic architectures minimize energy consumption by integrating memory and computation, as illustrated in the diagram below, which reduces the data transfer overhead prevalent in von Neumann architecture. The diagram shows how neuromorphic systems are efficient for parallel tasks due to their parallel processing capabilities, thereby reducing the need for additional power-intensive synchronization.
questions.rubric: Award 5 points for explaining how neuromorphic architectures reduce data transfer overhead, as shown in the diagram. Award 5 points for identifying parallel processing advantages, contributing to energy efficiency, as illustrated in the diagram below.
